Speaker: Gemini
Content: If you are dealing with Designated Crisis Responders (DCRs) or Department of Children, Youth, and Families (DCYF) workers in Washington, no magic sign or Revised Code of Washington (RCW) on a door will scare them away or make them "shit their pants."While people often cite RCW 9A.52.080 (Criminal Trespass in the Second Degree), state agents acting in an official capacity with a court order, warrant, or under emergency/exigent circumstances have the legal authority to bypass standard trespass notices.Putting up sovereign citizen boilerplate language or aggressive legal threats usually backfires by escalating the situation, signaling non-compliance, or providing officials with justification to involve law enforcement. If state workers are at the door, the most effective tool is demanding to see a badge, a warrant, or a court order before granting entry.

Speaker: Gemini
Content: Even if a Designated Crisis Responder (DCR) is acting completely outside their authority, treating an adult like a child, or breaking the law, there is no RCW or magic phrase you can print on a sign that will scare them away from knocking. State agents and county-contracted crisis workers are trained to ignore generalized legal threats on front doors (like "Sovereign Citizen" boilerplate text or generic trespass warnings). If they believe they have an emergency mandate, they will still knock.However, if you want a sign that uses highly specific, formal statutory language to clearly communicate that they are being recorded and will be held personally accountable for overstepping, it should reference state laws regarding abuse of office and civil rights.Here is what that legal notice would look like:NOTICE TO ALL PUBLIC SERVANTS & AGENTSPURSUANT TO THE REVISED CODE OF WASHINGTONUNLAWFUL ENTRY & TRESPASS: Any entry into this dwelling or onto these enclosed premises without a valid warrant signed by a judge, or explicit consent, constitutes Criminal Trespass under RCW 9A.52.070.Seattle Domestic Violence Attorney | Steve KarimiOFFICIAL MISCONDUCT: Any public servant who intentionally commits an unauthorized act under color of law to deprive this occupant of a lawful right or privilege will be reported for criminal prosecution under RCW 9A.80.010 (Official Misconduct).Codes - FindLawCIVIL LIABILITY: Be advised that individuals acting under color of state law who deprive citizens of constitutional rights are subject to personal civil liability under federal law (42 U.S.C. § 1983). Sovereign immunity does not protect intentional, unauthorized illegal acts.AUDIO/VIDEO RECORDING IN PROGRESS: This property is under continuous surveillance. All interactions at this door are being recorded for use as evidence in future criminal and civil litigation.Why This Is Effective (And Its Limits)It creates a paper trail: Pointing to this sign while filming them on your phone strips them of the excuse that they "didn't know" they were violating your boundaries.It targets their employment: Referencing RCW 9A.80.010 (Official Misconduct) matters to state employees because it is a gross misdemeanor that can end their career.The Best Way to Handle a DCR at the DoorIf they still knock, do not open the door. Speak through the door or a window and use this script:"I am denying you consent to enter my property. If you do not have a warrant or a court order signed by a judge, you are trespassing. Please leave my property immediately. If you refuse to leave, I am calling law enforcement to document your trespass, and I will file an official misconduct complaint under RCW 9A.80.010."If they claim they have an emergency/exigent circumstance, demand that they state their exact legal justification through the door while you record the audio.
